prose:
Method–provenance block
• Property: Condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, dual) for each atom in furan; most-electrophilic site reported here (f⁺ on atom 5).
• Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier ‘standard’), no density fitting (exact four-center integrals).
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (file `furan.xyz`), used as-is (single-point calculations on the fixed geometry; no re-optimization). Notation: DFT//input-XYZ.
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
• Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet). N ± 1 states: cation (+1, doublet) and anion (–1, doublet) were computed automatically for the Fukui analysis.
• Software: chemkit (PySCF backend) — functional ‘b3lyp’, basis ‘def2-tzvp’.
• Convergence & caveats: all three SCF calculations converged; integrity.status = “ok”, trustworthy = true. Engine warnings (verbatim):
  – sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
  – sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
  – common header present
  (The f⁺/f⁻ charge sums equal 1.0000, so these warnings are informational rather than indicating a problem.)
• Live log: calculation ran with live output streamed to `fukui_<timestamp>.out` in the working directory; you can `tail -f` this file for full SCF and analysis details.

Headline result
Most electrophilic site (highest f⁺): atom 5 (C) with f⁺ = 1.9242931 (dimensionless).

All per-atom Fukui indices, Mulliken charges of neutral/cation/anion states, and the bar-chart PNG are available in the saved JSON and plot files referenced above.
